name: AxonHub
tagline: Use any SDK to call any LLM, with failover, cost tracking, and request tracing built in
seoTitle: AxonHub: Open-Source AI Gateway for Any LLM
metaDescription: AxonHub is an open-source AI gateway that lets the OpenAI or Anthropic SDK call many LLM providers with failover, load balancing, cost tracking, and tracing.
overview: AxonHub is an open-source AI gateway that sits between your application and the LLM providers you use. You point your existing OpenAI or Anthropic SDK at AxonHub, and it translates each request so the same code can reach Claude, GPT, Gemini, and other supported models. Switching providers becomes a configuration change instead of a code rewrite.

It is aimed at teams that already call LLM APIs directly and want a single point of control. As an LLM gateway, it adds the operational layer that raw SDKs lack: automatic failover between channels, load balancing, per-request cost tracking, role-based access control, and end-to-end request tracing for debugging.

AxonHub is written in Go with a TypeScript and React frontend, and runs as a self-hosted service. You can start it from a release binary, Docker Compose, or a Helm chart, then send requests to its OpenAI-compatible endpoint at http://localhost:8090/v1.
features: Use the OpenAI SDK to call Claude, or the Anthropic SDK to call GPT, with no code changes
Smart load balancing with automatic failover to the healthiest channel
Full request tracing with thread-aware timelines for faster debugging
Real-time cost tracking with per-request breakdown of input, output, and cache tokens
Role-based access control with usage quotas and data isolation
Self-hosted deployment via release binary, Docker Compose, or Helm

gettingStarted.intro: Run AxonHub locally from a release binary, then call it with the standard OpenAI SDK pointed at its endpoint.

steps.heading: Download and run the binary
steps.body: Grab the latest release for your platform, extract it, and start the service. It listens on port 8090.

steps.heading: Open the dashboard
steps.body: Once running, open http://localhost:8090 to configure providers and create an API key.

steps.heading: Send a request through the gateway
steps.body: Point the OpenAI SDK at the AxonHub base URL and use your AxonHub API key. The same client can target models from different providers.
steps.code:
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    base_url="http://localhost:8090/v1",
    api_key="your-axonhub-api-key"
)

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}]
)

steps.heading: Deploy with Docker Compose (optional)
steps.body: For a server deployment, clone the repo and bring it up with Docker Compose.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/looplj/axonhub.git
cd axonhub
docker-compose up -d

useCases: Switch an app from one LLM provider to another without touching SDK code
Add failover and load balancing across multiple model channels for reliability
Track and control LLM spend with per-request cost breakdowns
Trace and debug LLM requests across a team with shared access control
